text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon in the quaint village of Everwood, there was a little old bookstore called "Whispering Leaves." The shop didn't do well by advertising. Its secret lay in the whispers it heard, ancient tales that danced on the winds. Every book lover knew that.

The shopkeeper was an old lady named Millie, whose eyes sparkled like stars. She knew the village like the back of her hand.

A young man named Ethan strolled in one rainy afternoon. He had heard whispers of Millie's wisdom. "Just pick something random," he told himself. He browsed through countless volumes, but nothing quite resonated with him.

Finally, he settled on a dusty, old-looking tome entitled *The Chronicles of Eldithia*. It looked quaint. He bought it on a whim.

As he began to read, the wind outside began to sing—a gentle lullaby carrying the voices of lost souls longing for home. Ethan found these whispers mesmerizing.

Days
